message:
chore(beads): file + close 3 conformance beads (fcntl, fenv, math)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-geoa","title":"resolv: getaddrinfo_a zero-item and null-request-list degenerate calls should succeed","description":"Host glibc returns success with errno unchanged for synchronous-degenerate getaddrinfo_a calls such as nitems=0 (even with a NULL list) and positive-length request arrays whose entries are all NULL. FrankenLibC currently returns EAI_SYSTEM/ENOSYS. Restore those no-op success semantics in unistd_abi.rs and lock them with focused ABI tests.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T06:27:52.529653096Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T06:30:33.157717821Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T06:30:33.155545909Z","close_reason":"Restored host-parity getaddrinfo_a success semantics for zero-item and all-NULL synchronous-degenerate request sets, with focused ABI regression coverage verified locally and via rch.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-gh8m4","title":"syscall: sched priority bounds for SCHED_DEADLINE should be zero exactly","description":"Host glibc returns min=0 and max=0 for sched_get_priority_{min,max}(SCHED_DEADLINE). Add focused ABI regression coverage so the Linux deadline-policy bounds contract is frozen exactly.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T09:33:47.405198256Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-22T04:42:33.365913964Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T04:42:33.363277112Z","close_reason":"Added sched_priority_bounds_sched_deadline_are_zero asserting min=max=0 host-parity for SCHED_DEADLINE (policy 6).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-gii3","title":"raise(0) incorrectly rejects POSIX null signal","description":"Our frankenlibc_abi::signal_abi::raise() checks valid_signal(sig) which requires sig in 1..=64. POSIX specifies raise(0) must succeed as the 'null signal' (no-op sending, checks tgkill permissions only). Host glibc's raise(0) does tgkill(pid,tid,0) which succeeds. Impacts gsignal(0) via raise(). Fix: add explicit null-signal handling to signal_abi::raise() before valid_signal check, calling tgkill(pid,tid,0) directly. Discovered while expanding signal_ops conformance fixtures (bd-9n4k).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T17:45:39.193198411Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T17:56:13.339368276Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T17:56:13.337394740Z","close_reason":"Implemented POSIX null-signal raise/gsignal semantics","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-gijn2","title":"REVIEW: [HIGH] inet_abi entry points walk unbounded user-supplied pointers (bd-z4k96 class)","description":"FOUND + FIXED via review round 5 (commit 0c0b59f1).

Four ABI entry points (inet_pton, inet_aton, inet_addr, if_nametoindex) dereferenced caller-supplied *const c_char via CStr::from_ptr with no length bound. A non-NUL-terminated argument walked arbitrary process memory until hitting an accidental zero byte — same info-leak / segfault class as bd-z4k96 (locale), cab4670a (iconv), 6f1e3036 (dlfcn).

Reachability: every program that parses an IP address from caller-controlled text (config readers, network-protocol parsers, getaddrinfo fallbacks) hits inet_pton/inet_aton/inet_addr; if_nametoindex is reached by every routing/iface-options call.

Fix: add read_bounded_cstr (mirrors locale/iconv/dlfcn copies) and replace each unbounded CStr::from_ptr; on None return the function's standard invalid-input sentinel.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cod-frankenlibc","created_at":"2026-04-24T07:45:00Z","created_by":"cod-frankenlibc","updated_at":"2026-04-24T07:45:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T07:45:00Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commits 0c0b59f1 / 1d68447a / fe3f1c06; 3120/3120 abi tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bd-z4k96-class","high","review","unbounded-cstr"]}
 {"id":"bd-gilq3","title":"[HIGH] bd15n2_fixture_gap_fill_artifacts_test fails: 'could not locate libfrankenlibc_abi.so'","description":"## Symptom

`cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test bd15n2_fixture_gap_fill_artifacts_test` fails:

```
thread 'fixture_gap_fill_gate_emits_valid_bd15n2_artifacts' panicked at
crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/bd15n2_fixture_gap_fill_artifacts_test.rs:42:5:
gate script failed:
stdout=
stderr=    Finished `release` profile [optimized] target(s) in 0.06s
FAIL: could not locate libfrankenlibc_abi.so
```

## Diagnosis

The test at line 42 invokes a gate script (presumably under scripts/) that needs the built `libfrankenlibc_abi.so` cdylib. The script builds a release profile of frankenlibc-abi (that step succeeds in the above log), then fails to *find* the resulting .so at the path it expects. The stderr only shows the cargo 'Finished release' line, no locator stderr from the script — the script likely greps `target/release` for the .so but we're running under `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc_cc` which the script doesn't respect.

## Fix direction

1. Read crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/bd15n2_fixture_gap_fill_artifacts_test.rs:42 and trace which gate script it spawns.
2. That script likely hardcodes `target/release/libfrankenlibc_abi.so`; change it to honour `CARGO_TARGET_DIR` (or at least look at `/data/tmp/cargo-target/release/libfrankenlibc_abi.so` when set) or fall back to `cargo metadata --format-version=1 | jq -r .target_directory`/`release/libfrankenlibc_abi.so`.

## Blast radius

Fails even in-isolation — test is always broken under a non-default `CARGO_TARGET_DIR`. Blocks the bd15n2 fixture-gap-fill gate from being exercised in any CI environment that redirects target_dir (rch, sccache, CI caches, etc.).

## Verification

- `cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test bd15n2_fixture_gap_fill_artifacts_test` — reproduces reliably with `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc_cc`.
- Expected to pass under default `CARGO_TARGET_DIR` (unset → defaults to `target/`).
","notes":"Fixed by cc-libc in the same push.

Root cause: scripts/bd15n2_fixture_gap_fill_run.sh maintained a hardcoded LIB_CANDIDATES array of three paths (target/release, target/debug, /data/tmp/cargo-target/release) and never considered a caller-supplied CARGO_TARGET_DIR. Under rch (which redirects to /tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc_cc) or any other non-default target-dir setup, the script's rebuild step put the .so somewhere the candidate list never looked.

Fix: append "${CARGO_TARGET_DIR}/release/libfrankenlibc_abi.so" and "${CARGO_TARGET_DIR}/debug/libfrankenlibc_abi.so" to LIB_CANDIDATES when the env var is set, matching the pattern that scripts/e2e_suite.sh already uses correctly.

Extended the same fix to scripts/c_fixture_suite.sh and scripts/ld_preload_smoke.sh, which had the identical bug — those are called by sibling gates that would have tripped next.

Also improved the error message to print the searched-path list when the library can't be located, so future breakages are diagnosable at a glance.

Validation: rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc_cc cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test bd15n2_fixture_gap_fill_artifacts_test -> 1 passed, 0 failed, 28.96s (worker vmi1152480).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cc-libc","created_at":"2026-04-22T13:18:06.994113107Z","created_by":"cc-libc","updated_at":"2026-04-22T13:46:11.806847Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T13:46:11.806847Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cdylib","full-review","harness","test-infra"]}

 {"id":"bd-ho3il","title":"[LOW] review: fprintf executor matrix lags snprintf coverage","description":"[LOW] review: fprintf executor matrix lags snprintf coverage

Recent printf pipeline changes expanded the snprintf/sprintf executor matrix, including `NullPtr` and several 3/4/5-argument combinations, but the fprintf path remains much narrower.

Current gap:
- `crates/frankenlibc_conformance/src/lib.rs:2712-2849` handles `PrintfArg::NullPtr` and multiple 3+/4+/5-argument combinations for `run_impl_snprintf` / `run_host_snprintf`.
- `crates/frankenlibc_conformance/src/lib.rs:2854-2910` still only supports the small fprintf subset and returns `unsupported fprintf arg combination` for anything wider.

That creates a coverage skew inside the same executor family: newly-added fixture cases can pass through snprintf/sprintf while equivalent fprintf cases are silently untestable.

Review ask:
- keep the fprintf dispatch matrix in lockstep with the snprintf/sprintf executor surface
- add at least the currently-supported `NullPtr` and 3+/4+/5-argument combinations so harness coverage does not regress by API shape","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:29:44.457254443Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T22:35:40.067098287Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T22:35:40.064806561Z","close_reason":"Verified parity: run_impl_snprintf/run_host_snprintf and run_impl_fprintf/run_host_fprintf matchers in crates/frankenlibc_conformance/src/lib.rs now carry identical arg-shape sets (17 match arms each; normalized diff shows EQUAL for both impl and host runners). NullPtr + 3/4/5-argument combinations present on both sides. Verified by:
  awk '/^fn run_impl_snprintf/,/^fn run_host_snprintf/' ... | grep -cE '^\s*\['  -> 17
  awk '/^fn run_impl_fprintf/,/^fn run_host_fprintf/' ... | grep -cE '^\s*\['    -> 17
  normalized sort/diff of arm shapes: EQUAL for both impl and host pairs.
End-to-end: rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_harness cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test printf_conformance_conformance_test -> 14/0 passed in 2.08s. Work landed in commits 1c5a9d0f + 9ea2ad56.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","coverage","printf","review"]}
+{"id":"bd-hxfu0","title":"REVIEW: [HIGH] execvp_via_execve walks unbounded user-supplied `file` (bd-z4k96 class)","description":"FOUND + FIXED via review round 5 (commit 1d68447a).

execvp_via_execve at L62 dereferenced the caller's `file` pointer via CStr::from_ptr with no length bound. A non-NUL-terminated pathname (attacker-controlled or memory-corrupted) walked arbitrary process memory.

Reachability: every program that resolves a command via PATH (shell builtins, system(), subprocess wrappers, container runtime exec helpers) hits this path with caller-supplied text.

Fix: read_bounded_cstr replacement; on None return -1 + ENOENT.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cod-frankenlibc","created_at":"2026-04-24T07:45:00Z","created_by":"cod-frankenlibc","updated_at":"2026-04-24T07:45:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T07:45:00Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commits 0c0b59f1 / 1d68447a / fe3f1c06; 3120/3120 abi tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bd-z4k96-class","high","review","unbounded-cstr"]}
 {"id":"bd-hzkc6","title":"syscall: signalfd4 existing fd update with same mask should return same fd without touching errno","description":"Host glibc returns the same fd and leaves errno unchanged for signalfd(existing_fd, &mask, 0). Add focused ABI regression coverage for the in-place update contract.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T10:56:03.717758642Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-22T05:06:51.635816783Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T05:06:51.633658460Z","close_reason":"signalfd4(existing_fd, same_mask, 0) → same_fd (in-place update) pinned.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-i3om4","title":"[HIGH] runtime_policy::ffi_pcc tests leak global state across parallel test threads","description":"## Symptom

`cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --lib` fails with:

```
thread 'runtime_policy::tests::ffi_pcc_decide_uses_certificate_gate_for_memcpy' panicked at crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/runtime_policy.rs:2168
test result: FAILED. 170 passed; 1 failed
```

Running the same test in isolation (`cargo test ... ffi_pcc_decide_uses_certificate_gate_for_memcpy -- --nocapture`) passes cleanly: '1 passed; 0 failed; 170 filtered out'.

## Diagnosis

The test already acquires `ffi_pcc_lock()` and calls `reset_ffi_pcc_state_for_tests`, `reset_decision_contract_machine_for_tests`, `enable_runtime_kernel_for_tests`, `set_mode_state_for_tests(MODE_HARDENED)`. That pattern suggests the author was aware of test-global-state coupling. The fact that the assertion at line 2168 (`assert_eq!(decision.policy_id, FFI_PCC_POLICY_BASE + 10)`) fires only under concurrent test scheduling means **at least one of those resets does not cover all mutable state that another test modifies concurrently** — either a static/lazy cache, a thread-local, or a singleton that is not inside the lock's scope.

Adjacent tests in the same module (`ffi_pcc_decide_uses_certificate_gate_for_snprintf` et al.) use the same lock and reset helpers and presumably race on the same shared state.

## Fix direction

1. Grep for every `static ATOMIC` / `LazyLock` / `thread_local` / `Mutex<` in runtime_policy.rs that the certificate gate path touches. Classify which are covered by `ffi_pcc_lock` vs `decision_contract_machine` vs neither.
2. Any state touched by `decide()` along the `lookup_active_ffi_pcc_certificate` -> `ffi_pcc_decision` path that is not inside one of the reset-helpers is a candidate leak.
3. Confirmed repro: run the full `cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --lib` twice in a row; the failure is sometimes the 'memcpy' test, sometimes 'snprintf' or another adjacent one — classic test-ordering-dependent leak.

## Blast radius

Blocks `cargo test --workspace`. Part of the same full-review batch as bd-g2bd5 (glibc_internal_abi_test heap corruption). Caught by cc-libc FULL REVIEW MODE.

## Verification

- `cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --lib runtime_policy::tests::ffi_pcc_decide_uses_certificate_gate_for_memcpy -- --nocapture` passes (1/1).
- `cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --lib` fails at the same test (170/171).
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cod-libc","created_at":"2026-04-22T13:17:44.580352654Z","created_by":"cc-libc","updated_at":"2026-04-22T13:32:50.585570161Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T13:32:50.583543353Z","close_reason":"Implemented by serializing runtime_policy test state and revalidating full abi lib suite","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["abi","full-review","race","runtime-policy","test"]}

 {"id":"bd-oai.4","title":"Dual-Mode: Unit tests - mode immutability, snapshot completeness, evidence integrity","description":"Unit tests: mode immutability (cannot switch after startup), snapshot completeness (all 154 fields populated), field range validation, monotone property tracking correctness, evidence record serialization roundtrip, RaptorQ redundancy verification (survive N% loss). Include property tests for deterministic replay.

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CobaltElm","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:01:54.820056192Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-15T07:22:04.653377909Z","closed_at":"2026-02-15T07:22:04.653300985Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["dual-mode","frankenlibc","test","unit-test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-oai.4","depends_on_id":"bd-oai","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1126,"issue_id":"bd-oai.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CobaltElm progress: added tests in crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/runtime_policy.rs and crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/runtime_math/mod.rs for mode immutability/reset parsing, snapshot field/literal/schema coherence, range checks, monotone decision+evidence counters, and decision-card JSON roundtrip. rch validation: cargo check workspace pass (with existing warnings), targeted runtime_policy and runtime_math tests pass, cargo clippy workspace fails due pre-existing unrelated frankenlibc-abi warnings/errors, and cargo test workspace fails on existing pthread_mutex_core_test failures unrelated to touched files.","created_at":"2026-02-15T07:13:14Z"},{"id":1127,"issue_id":"bd-oai.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress update (CobaltElm): added dual-mode unit-test coverage in  and snapshot/evidence integrity tests in .

New tests include:

- decision card JSON roundtrip preservation

Validation (rch-offloaded where CPU intensive):

Added tests in crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/runtime_policy.rs: mode stickiness until cache reset, cache re-parse after reset, and case-insensitive FRANKENLIBC_MODE alias parsing. Added tests in crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/runtime_math/mod.rs: snapshot struct/literal/schema coherence (>=154 baseline fields), snapshot range sanity, monotone decision/evidence counters, decision-card JSON roundtrip, deterministic replay equality across twin kernels, and RaptorQ-inspired redundancy budget/loss-fraction verification with deterministic repair payload generation.

rch validation commands and outcomes: cargo check -p frankenlibc-membrane -p frankenlibc-abi --all-targets PASS; cargo clippy -p frankenlibc-membrane --all-targets -- -D warnings PASS; cargo clippy -p frankenlibc-abi --all-targets -- -D warnings FAIL due pre-existing unrelated abi lint debt (pthread_abi/malloc_abi/stdio_abi/termios_abi/time_abi/locale_abi) plus one style lint in non-test runtime_policy path; cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi runtime_policy::tests PASS (11 passed); cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane runtime_math::tests::runtime_kernel_snapshot_schema_and_literal_cover_all_fields PASS; cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane runtime_math::tests::deterministic_replay_produces_identical_decisions_and_evidence PASS; cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane runtime_math::tests::raptorq_redundancy_budget_survives_target_loss_fraction PASS.

Representative failure signatures: abi clippy strict errors include unused-imports, unreachable-code, non_upper_case_globals, unnecessary-cast, let-and-return, and doc-lazy-continuation in files outside this bead scope.","created_at":"2026-02-15T07:20:32Z"},{"id":1129,"issue_id":"bd-oai.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Follow-up: resolved touched-file clippy style finding in crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/runtime_policy.rs by collapsing nested if in note_check_order_outcome. Re-ran via rch: cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi runtime_policy::tests PASS (11/11), cargo clippy -p frankenlibc-abi --all-targets -- -D warnings still fails only on pre-existing unrelated abi lint debt (21 errors remaining outside this bead).","created_at":"2026-02-15T07:21:31Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-oai.5","title":"Dual-Mode: E2E tests - deterministic replay, mode behavioral divergence, evidence chain","description":"E2E tests: deterministic replay (record session, replay with same inputs, verify identical outputs), mode behavioral divergence (same adversarial inputs produce different but both-correct results in strict vs hardened), evidence chain completeness (every hardened repair has evidence record, evidence chain is gapless and hash-linked).

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.

 {"id":"bd-oai.6","title":"Dual-Mode: Logging - mode selection, snapshot events, evidence emission","description":"Logging spec for dual-mode: TRACE for per-call mode dispatch decision, DEBUG for snapshot capture timing and field population, INFO for mode selection at startup and evidence record emission, WARN for snapshot field out-of-range values, ERROR for mode violation attempts (e.g., mode switch after startup). All records include trace_id+decision_id.

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"MagentaFalcon","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:05:42.187969197Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-25T06:09:45.353113266Z","closed_at":"2026-02-25T06:09:45.353015473Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["dual-mode","frankenlibc","logging","observability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-oai.6","depends_on_id":"bd-oai","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1131,"issue_id":"bd-oai.6","author":"MagentaFalcon","text":"2026-02-25 completion pass (MagentaFalcon): implemented dual-mode logging spec across runtime policy + runtime math export paths. In crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/runtime_policy.rs, added bounded JSONL mode-event log with trace_id+decision_id and events for startup selection (info), default strict fallback (info), mode resolution failure (error), resolution race/reentry (warn), and attempted mode switch after startup immutability (error). In crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/runtime_math/mod.rs, export_runtime_math_log_jsonl now emits runtime_mode_dispatch (trace, per decision), runtime_evidence_emitted (info), runtime_calibration with snapshot_capture_latency_ns + snapshot_validated_field_count (debug), runtime_snapshot (info), and runtime_snapshot_field_out_of_range (warn), all including trace/decision identifiers. Added/updated tests: mode_logging_captures_startup_selection_and_switch_attempt; runtime_math_log_jsonl_export_contains_required_runtime_decision_fields; snapshot_range_violations_identify_out_of_contract_fields. Verification (rch): cargo check -p frankenlibc-abi --all-targets (pass), cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi mode_ -- --nocapture (pass), cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane runtime_math_log_jsonl_export_contains_required_runtime_decision_fields -- --nocapture (pass), cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane snapshot_range_violations_identify_out_of_contract_fields -- --nocapture (pass). Remaining workspace fmt/clippy failures are pre-existing unrelated debt. Artifact refs: crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/runtime_policy.rs; crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/runtime_math/mod.rs","created_at":"2026-02-25T06:09:10Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-olgi","title":"Track B: Implement dlinfo for RTLD_DI_LINKMAP and RTLD_DI_ORIGIN","description":"## Goal
Replace the dlinfo stub in glibc_internal_abi.rs (line 4290) that returns ENOSYS with a real implementation supporting the two most commonly used request codes.

## Current State
```rust
pub unsafe extern "C" fn dlinfo(_handle: *mut c_void, _request: c_int, _info: *mut c_void) -> c_int {
    unsafe { *libc::__errno_location() = libc::ENOSYS };
    -1
}
```

## Why This Matters
Programs using dlinfo include:
- Python (ctypes.util.find_library -> RTLD_DI_LINKMAP to walk loaded libs)
- Ruby FFI, JVM, Lua FFI for plugin/library discovery
- Debugging tools (ltrace, strace analysis scripts)
- Programs checking RTLD_DI_ORIGIN to find their own install directory

## Supported Request Codes

### RTLD_DI_LINKMAP (2) — Write struct link_map* to info
The struct link_map (defined in <link.h>) has this layout:
```c
struct link_map {
    ElfW(Addr) l_addr;    // Base address of shared object
    char      *l_name;    // Absolute file name of shared object
    ElfW(Dyn) *l_ld;      // Dynamic section pointer
    struct link_map *l_next, *l_prev;  // Chain of loaded objects
};
```

Implementation:
- For RTLD_DEFAULT / NULL handle: return a static link_map for libfrankenlibc_abi.so itself. Populate l_addr and l_name from /proc/self/maps by finding our own library. l_ld can be null. l_next/l_prev = null (single entry).
- For other handles: delegate to host dlinfo via resolve_host_symbol_raw('dlinfo') if available. This is the interpose model — host-loaded libraries are the host linker's responsibility.
- If host delegation unavailable: return -1 with errno=ENOENT.

### RTLD_DI_ORIGIN (6) — Write directory path string to info (char* buffer)
- Parse /proc/self/maps to find the pathname for the handle's load address range.
- Extract dirname (everything up to last '/').
- Copy into info buffer (caller must provide sufficient space — POSIX says PATH_MAX).
- For RTLD_DEFAULT: return dirname of libfrankenlibc_abi.so.
- For unknown handles: delegate to host or return -1 with EINVAL.

### Other request codes: Return -1 with errno=ENOSYS
RTLD_DI_SERINFO (4), RTLD_DI_SERINFOSIZE (5), RTLD_DI_TLS_MODID (9), RTLD_DI_TLS_DATA (10) — rare, remain ENOSYS.

## Design Details

### Self-identification
We already have /proc/self/maps parsing in host_resolve.rs (loaded_libc_base_and_path). Reuse that to find our own library path. Cache the result in a static OnceLock<(u64, String)>.

### Handle classification
- NULL or RTLD_DEFAULT (pseudo-handle): self
- Address within our .so's mapped range: self
- Otherwise: delegate to host dlinfo

### Static link_map for self
Allocate a static link_map struct (leaked Box) on first call. Populate with:
- l_addr = our base address from /proc/self/maps
- l_name = leaked CString of our library path
- l_ld = null (we don't expose our own dynamic section)
- l_next = l_prev = null

## Files Changed
- crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/glibc_internal_abi.rs: Replace dlinfo body (~60 lines)
- May add a helper fn in host_resolve.rs for self-identification

## Unit Tests (in tests/glibc_internal_abi_test.rs or tests/dlfcn_abi_test.rs)
- test dlinfo_origin_default_returns_valid_directory: dlinfo(RTLD_DEFAULT, RTLD_DI_ORIGIN, buf) writes a non-empty path that is a real directory (stat it)
- test dlinfo_origin_null_handle_returns_directory: dlinfo(NULL, RTLD_DI_ORIGIN, buf) same as above
- test dlinfo_linkmap_default_returns_nonnull: dlinfo(RTLD_DEFAULT, RTLD_DI_LINKMAP, &ptr) writes a non-null link_map* with valid l_name
- test dlinfo_linkmap_l_name_is_valid_path: the l_name in the returned link_map is a readable file path
- test dlinfo_unsupported_request_returns_enosys: dlinfo(NULL, 999, ptr) returns -1, errno=ENOSYS
- test dlinfo_bad_handle_returns_error: dlinfo(0xDEADBEEF, RTLD_DI_ORIGIN, buf) returns -1

## e2e Test Script: scripts/check_dlinfo_native.sh
Build and run a C test program under LD_PRELOAD that:
1. Calls dlinfo(RTLD_DEFAULT, RTLD_DI_ORIGIN, buf) and prints origin
2. Calls dlinfo(RTLD_DEFAULT, RTLD_DI_LINKMAP, &lm) and prints l_name
3. Verifies both return 0
4. Emits structured JSONL report

## Structured Logging
dlinfo emits: trace_id, api_family=dlfcn, symbol=dlinfo, request_code, handle_class (self|host|unknown), result, errno, latency_ns

## Acceptance Criteria
- dlinfo handles RTLD_DI_LINKMAP and RTLD_DI_ORIGIN for default/self handles
- Host delegation for non-self handles when host dlinfo available
- ENOSYS for unsupported request codes, EINVAL for bad handles
- 6+ unit tests pass
- e2e script passes under LD_PRELOAD
- cargo clippy clean
- LD_PRELOAD smoke suite not regressed","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:17:50.994367570Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:48:34.754188981Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T02:48:34.753906196Z","close_reason":"Implemented dlinfo for RTLD_DI_LINKMAP and RTLD_DI_ORIGIN. Self-identification via /proc/self/maps (cached in OnceLock). RTLD_DI_LINKMAP: returns static leaked link_map with base addr and pathname for default/self handle, delegates to host for other handles. RTLD_DI_ORIGIN: extracts dirname from library path. Unsupported requests return ENOSYS. Clippy clean, LD_PRELOAD smoke passes.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["dlfcn","native","stub-elimination"],"comments":[{"id":1362,"issue_id":"bd-olgi","author":"ubuntu","text":"dlinfo is a GNU extension (not POSIX). Key use cases: (1) RTLD_DI_LINKMAP - Python ctypes, Ruby FFI, JVM enumerate loaded libs. For interpose model, delegate to host dlinfo for host handles, return minimal struct for our own. (2) RTLD_DI_ORIGIN - directory of shared object from /proc/self/maps. RTLD_DI_SERINFO/SERINFOSIZE/TLS_MODID are rare, can remain ENOSYS.","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:21:01Z"}]}

-{"id":"bd-gijn2","title":"REVIEW: [HIGH] inet_abi entry points walk unbounded user-supplied pointers (bd-z4k96 class)","description":"FOUND + FIXED via review round 5 (commit 0c0b59f1).

Four ABI entry points (inet_pton, inet_aton, inet_addr, if_nametoindex) dereferenced caller-supplied *const c_char via CStr::from_ptr with no length bound. A non-NUL-terminated argument walked arbitrary process memory until hitting an accidental zero byte — same info-leak / segfault class as bd-z4k96 (locale), cab4670a (iconv), 6f1e3036 (dlfcn).

Reachability: every program that parses an IP address from caller-controlled text (config readers, network-protocol parsers, getaddrinfo fallbacks) hits inet_pton/inet_aton/inet_addr; if_nametoindex is reached by every routing/iface-options call.

Fix: add read_bounded_cstr (mirrors locale/iconv/dlfcn copies) and replace each unbounded CStr::from_ptr; on None return the function's standard invalid-input sentinel.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cod-frankenlibc","created_at":"2026-04-24T07:45:00.000000000Z","created_by":"cod-frankenlibc","updated_at":"2026-04-24T07:45:00.000000000Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T07:45:00.000000000Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commits 0c0b59f1 / 1d68447a / fe3f1c06; 3120/3120 abi tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["review","high","bd-z4k96-class","unbounded-cstr"]}
-{"id":"bd-hxfu0","title":"REVIEW: [HIGH] execvp_via_execve walks unbounded user-supplied `file` (bd-z4k96 class)","description":"FOUND + FIXED via review round 5 (commit 1d68447a).

execvp_via_execve at L62 dereferenced the caller's `file` pointer via CStr::from_ptr with no length bound. A non-NUL-terminated pathname (attacker-controlled or memory-corrupted) walked arbitrary process memory.

Reachability: every program that resolves a command via PATH (shell builtins, system(), subprocess wrappers, container runtime exec helpers) hits this path with caller-supplied text.

Fix: read_bounded_cstr replacement; on None return -1 + ENOENT.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cod-frankenlibc","created_at":"2026-04-24T07:45:00.000000000Z","created_by":"cod-frankenlibc","updated_at":"2026-04-24T07:45:00.000000000Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T07:45:00.000000000Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commits 0c0b59f1 / 1d68447a / fe3f1c06; 3120/3120 abi tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["review","high","bd-z4k96-class","unbounded-cstr"]}
